CREATE TABLE geo_points_g1 (id INT, point GEOMETRY);
CREATE TABLE geo_points_g2 (id INT, point GEOMETRY);
INSERT INTO geo_points_g1 (id, point) VALUES (1, ST_POINT(0, 52));
INSERT INTO geo_points_g1 (id, point) VALUES (2, ST_POINT(0, 70));
INSERT INTO geo_points_g2 (id, point) VALUES (1, ST_POINT(1, 53));
INSERT INTO geo_points_g2 (id, point) VALUES (2, ST_POINT(1, 72));
SELECT ST_DISTANCE(g1.point, g2.point) AS distance FROM geo_points_g1 g1, geo_points_g2 g2 WHERE g1.id = 1 AND g2.id = 1;
SELECT ST_DISTANCE(g1.point, g2.point) FROM (SELECT ST_POINT(0, 52) AS point) AS g1, (SELECT ST_POINT(1, 53) AS point) AS g2;
SELECT g1.id, ST_DISTANCE(g1.point, g2.point) AS distance FROM geo_points_g1 g1 JOIN geo_points_g2 g2 ON ST_DISTANCE(g1.point, g2.point) < 100;
SELECT g1.id, g2.id, ST_DISTANCE(g1.point, g2.point) AS distance FROM geo_points_g1 g1 CROSS JOIN geo_points_g2 g2;
SELECT (SELECT ST_DISTANCE(g1.point, g2.point) FROM geo_points_g1 g1 LIMIT 1) AS first_distance, (SELECT ST_DISTANCE(g1.point, g2.point) FROM geo_points_g2 g2 LIMIT 1) AS second_distance;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS count, ST_DISTANCE(g1.point, g2.point) AS distance FROM geo_points_g1 g1, geo_points_g2 g2 GROUP BY distance HAVING distance > 10;
